description:
You are a DevOps engineer who has just received a complaint that several errors occurred on a specific day and you need to prepare a concise summary for your team.

The log file is already present at  
/home/user/logs/app.log  

Each line in the file follows this fixed structure (single space between every field):

YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS LEVEL MESSAGE

Example (do **not** assume the times below are the only ones in the file):

2023-08-15 08:33:48 ERROR Timeout while reading configuration

Objective  
1. Using a single `awk` **or** `sed` command piped (if desired) to a redirect, extract every line that  
   • has the date 2023-08-15, **and**  
   • has the log level ERROR.  
2. From those matching lines, keep only the “time” field (HH:MM:SS) and the full MESSAGE portion, separated by **one horizontal tab character** (U+0009).  
3. Write the resulting lines to a new file named  
   /home/user/logs/2023-08-15-errors.txt  

Required output format inside /home/user/logs/2023-08-15-errors.txt (exactly one tab between the two columns, no extra spaces, no extra lines at start or end):

HH:MM:SS<TAB>The original message text

For instance, if the time were 08:33:48 and the message were “Timeout while reading configuration”, the line in the summary file must be:

08:33:48<TAB>Timeout while reading configuration

Deliverables the automated checker will verify:  
• The file /home/user/logs/2023-08-15-errors.txt exists.  
• It contains only the ERROR entries for date 2023-08-15, formatted exactly as described, one entry per line, in the same order they appeared in app.log.  
• No other files are created or modified.

You may inspect /home/user/logs/app.log to understand its contents, but accomplish the task with a single `awk` or `sed` command (you may include pattern-matching or simple field re-arrangement as needed).
truth:
Initial state before the agent begins:

File: /home/user/logs/app.log  
Contents (exact lines, with trailing newline at the end of the file):

2023-08-14 10:15:23 INFO User login success
2023-08-14 10:17:01 ERROR Failed to connect to DB
2023-08-15 07:02:11 WARN Disk usage high
2023-08-15 08:33:48 ERROR Timeout while reading configuration
2023-08-15 09:01:10 ERROR Invalid user input
2023-08-15 14:22:59 ERROR Connection reset by peer
2023-08-16 12:45:00 INFO Routine maintenance started

(The file ends with a single trailing newline after “started”.)

Desired final state after the agent finishes:

1. A new file /home/user/logs/2023-08-15-errors.txt exists, permissions 644 (-rw-r--r--).  
2. The file has exactly three lines, each ending with a single newline character, in the following exact order and spelling:

08:33:48\tTimeout while reading configuration
09:01:10\tInvalid user input
14:22:59\tConnection reset by peer

(\t represents a literal tab; there are no spaces before or after it.)

3. /home/user/logs/app.log remains unaltered (checksum identical to the one recorded at start).
name: task_000000_9b4fb033
